# Main & Machine

> Human-centric AI implementation for small and mid-size business. We build working agents, automations, and data integrations into the work a company already does, at a fixed price and delivered in about ninety days. The technology does the tireless part; a person stays accountable for every decision.

Main & Machine is an AI implementation firm, not a strategy shop or a reseller. We go into a business, find the few workflows quietly costing the most, and build the systems that fix them. Every system we put in place can be explained, questioned, and overruled by a human who owns the outcome. Based in Denver and Phoenix, working remotely across the US.

## What we do

- Custom agents: software that handles the repetitive decisions and drafts the hard ones, then routes the judgment call back to a person.
- Automations and workflow: connective tissue between the tools a business already pays for, so work moves on its own.
- Integrations and data: one source of truth instead of five systems that do not talk to each other.

## Services and pricing

- [AI Readiness Audit](https://www.mainandmachine.com/#pricing): $3,500 to $8,500, 2 to 4 weeks. A workflow map, the handful of places AI actually pays, a phased plan you own outright, and a straight read on whether to build at all.
- [AI Implementation Sprint](https://www.mainandmachine.com/#pricing): $12,000 to $45,000, 4 to 12 weeks. Working agents, automations, and integrations tested inside your real operation, with your team trained to run them. Fixed quote in writing after scoping; the price does not move after work begins.
- [Managed Services](https://www.mainandmachine.com/#pricing): monthly retainer, ongoing and optional. Monitoring, maintenance as models and vendors shift, and new automations as the work changes. No lock-in.
- [Free AI Opportunity Assessment](https://www.mainandmachine.com/book/): a free 30-minute call. A senior advisor walks your workflows and tells you where AI pays, where it does not, and what it is worth. No obligation, no pitch.

## Who it is for

Small and mid-size businesses past the point where the owner can hold everything in their head, and not yet large enough for a Fortune 500 budget. Typically 5 to 100 people and $1M to $50M in revenue. Clearest wins seen in five industries: professional services (law, accounting, insurance, consulting, financial advisory), retail and e-commerce, healthcare and wellness, construction and trades, and hospitality and food service.

## Founder

Christopher Myers, founder and chairman. CEO of B:Side Capital and Fund, professor of entrepreneurship at ASU's W.P. Carey School, and author of several books on small business. Twenty-six years backing Main Street. His work has been covered in Forbes, The Wall Street Journal, The New York Times, Inc., TechCrunch, MSNBC, and Fox Business.

## Contact

- Email: cmyers@mainandmachine.com
- Phone: 480-360-5128
- Locations: Denver, Colorado and Phoenix, Arizona (remote across the US)
